types.name: Course

types.name: OpenClaw Getting started
types.description: Starter materials introduce local-first agent workflows and the core operational patterns behind the framework.

properties.example: Starter materials introduce local-first agent workflows and the core operational patterns behind the framework.

types.description: Guide to installing OpenClaw in bash or windows powershell.

types.description: Choose an onboarding path (CLI wizard or macOS app) to set up the Gateway and providers, including custom endpoints if needed.

types.description: Those who supply a product or service.

types.name: Install tooling
types.description: The software and utilities required to manage, build, and run a specific application.

properties.example: The software and utilities required to manage, build, and run a specific application.

types.name: Verify environment
types.description: The pre-check to ensure your system meets the software's requirements before you attempt to run it.

types.name: Governance manager
types.description: Owns the “rules of the road” for AI in an organization, setting governance frameworks and policies, tracking AI inventory and risks and ensuring compliance with laws and standards.

properties.example: Owns the “rules of the road” for AI in an organization, setting governance frameworks and policies, tracking AI inventory and risks and ensuring compliance with laws and standards.

types.name: Basic monitoring
types.description: The foundational system that provides visibility into whether a system is alive and performing as expected.

properties.example: The foundational system that provides visibility into whether a system is alive and performing as expected.

types.name: LLM Deployment
types.description: Research topic (Topic).

types.name: Technical product manager
types.description: A strategic leader who bridges complex engineering and business goals by translating technical constraints into product value and ensuring feasibility.

properties.example: A strategic leader who bridges complex engineering and business goals by translating technical constraints into product value and ensuring feasibility.

types.name: Lifecycle
types.description: The iterative journey of a model from its initial design to its eventual replacement.

properties.example: The iterative journey of a model from its initial design to its eventual replacement.

types.name: Automation
types.description: The process of using technology to perform repetitive, predictable tasks without manual human intervention.

properties.example: The process of using technology to perform repetitive, predictable tasks without manual human intervention.

types.name: Platform Engineer
types.description: Builds internal developer platforms, tooling, and infrastructure that other engineers build on top of.

properties.example: Builds internal developer platforms, tooling, and infrastructure that other engineers build on top of.

types.name: Security configuration
types.description: The process of hardening a system by defining its defensive boundaries and access rules.

properties.example: The process of hardening a system by defining its defensive boundaries and access rules.

types.name: Evaluation engineer
types.description: The quality assurance specialist for AI, designing tests and metrics to measure AI performance.

properties.example: The quality assurance specialist for AI, designing tests and metrics to measure AI performance.

types.name: LLMOps engineer
types.description: Manages the entire lifecycle of LLMs in production, including deployment, monitoring, scaling, and, most importantly, optimizing for unpredictable behavior.

properties.example: Manages the entire lifecycle of LLMs in production, including deployment, monitoring, scaling, and, most importantly, optimizing for unpredictable behavior.

types.name: OpenClaw
types.description: Any OS gateway for AI agents across WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, iMessage, and more.

properties.example: Any OS gateway for AI agents across WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, iMessage, and more.

types.description: Distinct steps in a process, shown in order from start to finish

types.name: Foundations
types.description: The underlying infrastructure, core principles, and prerequisite knowledge upon which a system is built.

properties.example: The underlying infrastructure, core principles, and prerequisite knowledge upon which a system is built.

types.name: Install
types.description: The process of transferring, unpacking, and placing the core components into a system so that it functions.

types.name: Setup
types.description: The transitional phase where you move from having the raw inputs to having a functional system.

types.name: Governance
types.description: Policies, controls, and oversight ensuring AI is safe, compliant, reliable, transparent, and accountable.

properties.example: Policies, controls, and oversight ensuring AI is safe, compliant, reliable, transparent, and accountable.

types.name: Configure authorization
types.description: The ability to programmatically authorize one system to securely communicate with another using industry standard methods like oauth or security keys.

properties.example: The ability to programmatically authorize one system to securely communicate with another using industry standard methods like oauth or security keys.

types.name: Gateway managament
types.description: The ongoing gateway operation to configure routing and access, manage updates and uptime, monitor, secure secrets, troubleshoot failures.

properties.example: The ongoing gateway operation to configure routing and access, manage updates and uptime, monitor, secure secrets, troubleshoot failures.

types.name: Deployment
types.description: Packaging, releasing, and operating AI models in real environments with monitoring, scaling, and updates.
